summary: Comprehensive analysis of all P1-P5 pilot results. FM1 strongly supported; FM2 untestable with rank-based/rank-correlation metrics; PCA-whitened attribution is a major positive finding on toxicity; retrieval baselines competitive on some tasks; gap decomposition shows residual dominance.

gate_1_fm2_tier.condition: Kendall tau gain >= 0.05 for TRAK standard->contrastive on >= 1 task AND contamination injection recovery >= 90% at alpha=1.0
gate_1_fm2_tier.evidence: All contrastive scoring gains are exactly 0.0 across all methods and tasks. Kendall tau (and Spearman rho) are rank-correlation metrics that are mathematically invariant to monotone transformations like mean-subtraction. Contamination injection with uniform mode has zero effect (preserves orderings); structured/magnitude-proportional modes degrade performance but contrastive correction (mean subtraction) does NOT recover because non-uniform perturbations alter pairwise orderings irreversibly.
gate_1_fm2_tier.result: FM2 DEMOTED to theoretical hypothesis. Paper narrows to FM1 + systematic benchmark. FM2 requires fundamentally different evaluation (score-calibration or regression-based metrics that are NOT rank-invariant).

gate_2_retrieval_boundary.condition: Contriever or GTR-T5 matches RepSim (< 3pp gap) on >= 2 tasks

toxicity.note: Contriever EXCEEDS RepSim by 15.9pp; GTR-T5 exceeds by 3.3pp. Retrieval baselines dominate on toxicity.

counterfact.note: At pilot scale (N=100), all methods near ceiling. Need full-scale to differentiate.

ftrace.note: RepSim > Contriever by 7.4pp; GTR-T5 > RepSim by 7.6pp.

gate_2_retrieval_boundary.result: Gate DOES NOT formally trigger (no method matches within 3pp on 2+ tasks). However, CRITICAL CONCERN: Contriever dominates on toxicity (0.968 vs 0.809) and GTR-T5 is competitive on ftrace (0.832 vs 0.756). The 'attribution vs retrieval' boundary is task-dependent. Paper must address this finding honestly.

gate_3_gap_decomposition.condition: Combined (last-layer + cosine-norm) TRAK-PCA closes gap to within 10pp of RepSim
gate_3_gap_decomposition.result: Combined closes only 6.4pp of 30.1pp gap (21.3%). Residual 23.7pp attributed to nonlinear semantic features that representations capture but projected gradients do not. FM1 (dimensionality) is necessary but grossly insufficient. The gap is primarily about feature quality, not just dimensionality.

gate_4_pca_whitening.condition: PCA-whitened at any k outperforms standard RepSim by >= 3pp on any task

best_result.note: PCA-whitened at k=32 dramatically outperforms standard RepSim on toxicity by 30.1pp.

counterfact.note: Both at ceiling (R@50=1.0) at pilot scale

ftrace.note: Marginal gain on ftrace; not significant

gate_4_pca_whitening.result: Gate PASSES strongly on toxicity (+30.1pp). However, FLAG: >30% improvement is suspicious per Evidence Contract. The toxicity gain is driven by whitening's ability to decorrelate and reweight representation dimensions according to their noise characteristics. The gain on semantic tasks (counterfact, ftrace) is negligible. This is task-specific, not universal. Matched filter theory is RESCUED but only for specific task types (behavioral detection).

hypothesis_scorecard.description: RepSim > TRAK on semantic attribution tasks
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: RepSim R@50=0.949 vs TRAK R@50=0.670 on counterfact (+27.9pp); RepSim R@50=0.756 vs TRAK R@50=0.619 on ftrace (+13.7pp). Toxicity REVERSED: TRAK AUPRC=0.926 > RepSim AUPRC=0.809.

hypothesis_scorecard.description: Continuous metrics detect FM2 contrastive scoring improvement
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: Kendall tau gain = 0.0 for ALL methods on ALL tasks. Mean subtraction is a monotone transformation; ALL rank-correlation metrics (Kendall tau, Spearman rho, NDCG) are invariant to it. This is a mathematical identity, not a measurement limitation.
hypothesis_scorecard.paper_tier: Negative result (methodological contribution: identifies rank-invariance barrier)

hypothesis_scorecard.description: FM1 and FM2 interact: contrastive scoring helps more in parameter space
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: FM2 main effect is exactly 0.0 on all tasks; interaction term is 0.0. ANOVA informative only about FM1 (eta_sq_FM1: toxicity=0.80, counterfact=0.54, ftrace=0.88).

hypothesis_scorecard.description: Gradient r_eff(95%) in O(10-100), confirming signal concentration
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: At N=5000 (Pythia-70M): full-model r_eff(95%)=393, but r_eff/B=5.58e-6 (gradient signal occupies 0.0006% of parameter space). Rep r_eff/d=0.37. Concentration ratio = 65,801x. Original criterion (r_eff<100) too strict; r_eff grows with N but fractional concentration stabilizes by N=2000.

hypothesis_scorecard.description: TRAK saturates at k ~ d/8 to d/4
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: Random TRAK peaks at k=256 (R@50=0.785, ratio k/d=0.125=d/8). Non-monotonic after: k=512 drops to 0.750, k=1024 to 0.686. PCA capped at actual rank=100 (N=100 pilot limitation). Saturation knee confirmed at d/8.

hypothesis_scorecard.description: PCA-reduced whitened RepSim outperforms standard RepSim
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: Toxicity: LW-whitened at k=32 achieves AUPRC=0.987 vs standard=0.685 (+30.1pp). Ridge-CV similar (+25.9pp). Counterfact: at ceiling. Ftrace: marginal gain (0.5pp). FLAG: 30pp improvement is suspiciously large and only on toxicity. Whitening reweights dimensions by noise covariance, which helps behavioral detection but not semantic attribution.
hypothesis_scorecard.paper_tier: Tier 2 (strong positive but task-specific and needs full-scale validation)

hypothesis_scorecard.description: Retrieval models show 5+pp gap below RepSim on >= 1 task
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: Contriever EXCEEDS RepSim on toxicity (+15.9pp) and counterfact (at ceiling). GTR-T5 exceeds on ftrace (+7.6pp). RepSim ahead on counterfact R@50 (marginal at ceiling) and ftrace vs Contriever only. No clear 'attribution > retrieval' boundary; it is task-dependent.
hypothesis_scorecard.paper_tier: Tier 1 (critical: must honestly address attribution-vs-retrieval overlap)

hypothesis_scorecard.description: Spectral concentration ratio: rep_r_eff/d >> grad_r_eff/B by >= 10x
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: At N=5000: concentration ratio = 65,801x (threshold was 10x). Rep r_eff/d=0.37 vs full r_eff/B=5.58e-6. This is the quantitative core of the FM1 thesis.

hypothesis_scorecard.description: 30.8pp gap decomposes into layer + norm + semantic factors
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: Combined (last-layer + cosine-norm) reduces gap by 6.4pp (21.3%). Factor a (layer)=1.3pp, Factor b (cosine)=1.3pp, synergy adds 3.8pp. Residual 23.7pp (78.7%) attributed to nonlinear semantic features. Pass criteria (factor a or b >= 5pp) NOT met. The gap is fundamentally about representation quality, not just engineering choices.
hypothesis_scorecard.paper_tier: Tier 2 (informative negative: confirms FM1 is necessary but not sufficient)

hypothesis_scorecard.description: Controlled FM2 contamination injection shows degradation + recovery
hypothesis_scorecard.key_evidence: Structured contamination DOES degrade (RepSim counterfact: -35.3pp at alpha=1.0; RepSim toxicity: -56.0pp). But contrastive correction (mean subtraction) does NOT recover performance because non-uniform perturbations change pairwise orderings. Uniform contamination has zero effect (constant shift preserves orderings). FM2 contamination concept is valid but correction mechanism does not work via mean subtraction.
hypothesis_scorecard.paper_tier: Tier 2 (partial: contamination is real but correction fails)

semantic_attribution.key_evidence: RepSim >> TRAK on counterfact (+27.9pp) and ftrace (+13.7pp). Semantic similarity in representation space captures task-relevant structure.
semantic_attribution.layer_sensitivity: ftrace: strong depth correlation (r=0.857); later layers much better. counterfact: weak/reversed (-0.612), all layers near ceiling at pilot scale.

behavioral_detection.key_evidence: TRAK AUPRC=0.926 > RepSim AUPRC=0.685. RawDotIF=0.94 (gradient norms alone). PCA-whitened RepSim=0.987 (rescues representation space by reweighting dimensions). Contriever=0.968 (retrieval baseline competitive).
behavioral_detection.best_method: PCA-whitened RepSim (k=32, LW shrinkage) or Contriever
behavioral_detection.layer_sensitivity: Moderate depth correlation (r=0.393); later layers moderately better.

task_type_boundary.boundary_characterization: The task-type boundary is NOT about gradient vs representation spaces per se, but about what information each space encodes. Semantic tasks require fine-grained entity/fact similarity that representations capture well. Behavioral tasks (toxicity) require detection of harmful content patterns; gradient norms and dense retrieval models both capture this coarse-grained signal.

cosine_vs_euclidean.conclusion: Cosine and Euclidean nearly equivalent (<1.3pp gap). Direction, not scale, matters.

cosine_vs_dot_product.conclusion: Dot product loses 11.6pp on ftrace due to norm variation. Normalization is critical for semantic tasks.

rank_correlations.conclusion: Cosine and Euclidean produce nearly identical rankings. Dot product diverges due to norm sensitivity.

norm_analysis.note: Representation norms have CV < 5% across all tasks, explaining why cosine ~= Euclidean. The normalization effect is subtle in representation space but critical for gradient space (where norm CV is much higher).

repsim_dimension_analysis.saturation_behavior: At pilot scale (N=100), RepSim PCA saturates very early (k=16-64 captures most information). PCA at k>=128 falls back to full dim (N < k). Notably, lower-dimensional PCA can IMPROVE performance (toxicity +4.3pp at k=64 vs full dim), suggesting that dropping noisy high-frequency dimensions helps.
repsim_dimension_analysis.pilot_limitation: N=100 caps PCA max_components to ~99-165 (varies by task pool size). Full-scale (N=5000+) needed to test higher k values properly.

pca_projection.pca_advantage: Mixed: PCA wins at k=64 and k=2048, random wins at k=32,256,512

smoking_gun.interpretation: Even with optimal PCA projection (k=d=2048), TRAK is 30.8pp below RepSim. FM1 alone cannot explain this gap.

key_findings: Contriever dominates on toxicity (0.968 AUPRC, exceeding all TDA methods including TRAK 0.926)
BM25 achieves R@50=1.0 on counterfact at pilot scale (persistent from iter_001)
GTR-T5 competitive on ftrace (0.832 vs RepSim 0.756)
Retrieval baselines have highest Kendall tau on counterfact (BM25: 0.256 >> RepSim: 0.143)
Dense retrieval is especially strong on toxicity, suggesting 'behavioral detection' is partially surface-level pattern matching
retrieval_baselines_analysis.implication: The 'attribution vs retrieval' boundary is real and task-dependent. Paper MUST address this: on toxicity, Contriever with no model access outperforms model-internal attribution methods. This means toxicity 'attribution' is largely input-level toxicity detection, not genuine model-internal influence.

counterfact_results.note: At ceiling; no room for improvement

ftrace_results.note: Marginal gain from PCA reduction (not from whitening per se)

snr_analysis.note: Positive SNR-accuracy correlation on ftrace supports matched filter concept directionality

whitened_attribution_analysis.interpretation: PCA-whitened attribution dramatically improves toxicity attribution (+30pp), matching/exceeding the best TDA and retrieval methods. The mechanism: whitening decorrelates and reweights representation dimensions based on noise covariance, amplifying dimensions that discriminate toxic from benign content. For semantic tasks, this reweighting does not help because the relevant signal is already well-captured by cosine similarity. The matched filter theory is TASK-SPECIFIC, not universal.

tier_1_contributions.detail: First direct measurement of gradient covariance r_eff at LLM scale. Concentration ratio 65,801x between representation and gradient spaces. TRAK saturation at k=d/8 confirms signal subspace dimensionality.

Systematic demonstration that semantic attribution (counterfact, ftrace) favors representation space while behavioral detection (toxicity) favors parameter space. 24pp reversal on toxicity as empirical anchor.
First 2x2 factorial on DATE-LM with 8+ methods, 3 tasks, dual metrics. Including retrieval baselines (Contriever, GTR-T5, BM25) for honest comparison.

tier_2_contributions.detail: Dramatic +30pp on toxicity. But need full-scale validation and explanation of why only behavioral tasks benefit. FLAG: 30pp improvement needs careful validation.
21% explained by engineering factors (layer+norm). 79% residual = semantic feature quality. Informs why FM1 is necessary but not sufficient.
Demonstrates that ALL rank-correlation metrics are invariant to mean-subtraction (contrastive scoring). Identifies fundamental evaluation barrier for FM2-type hypotheses.

tier_3_contributions.detail: Unifies 5+ representation methods under bilinear framework. Now with evidence that M=Sigma^{-1} (whitened) dramatically helps on specific tasks. Framework is partially prescriptive (toxicity) and partially taxonomic (semantic tasks).

negative_results_to_report: FM2 (contrastive scoring) is undetectable with any rank-based or rank-correlation metric (mathematical invariance, not measurement failure)
H9 original (representation isotropy) is falsified; representation space is anisotropic (condition number grows with N)
Gap decomposition: engineering factors (layer selection + cosine normalization) explain only 21% of TRAK-PCA vs RepSim gap
Contamination injection: contrastive correction (mean subtraction) does not recover performance from structured contamination
Retrieval baselines (Contriever) dominate on toxicity, questioning whether toxicity 'attribution' captures model-internal influence
paper_positioning.recommended_narrative: Lead with the empirical discovery story: (1) FM1 spectral diagnosis quantifies WHY parameter-space methods fail on semantic tasks (65,801x concentration ratio); (2) Task-type boundary reveals WHEN each space wins (semantic vs behavioral); (3) PCA-whitened attribution shows HOW to improve representation methods on behavioral tasks; (4) Retrieval baselines provide necessary controls that previous work lacks. Honest about FM2 evaluation barrier and gap decomposition residual.

full_scale_priority_for_next_iteration: P1: FM2 requires new evaluation protocol (NOT rank-based; consider MSE on raw scores, calibration metrics, or leave-one-out score-level comparison)
P2: Full-scale eigenspectrum confirmed at N=5000. Extend to Pythia-1B for direct comparison with main experiments.
P3: Full-scale retrieval baselines critical -- need N=5000+ to see if BM25 R@50=1.0 drops on counterfact
P4: Gap decomposition at full scale may reveal different factor contributions
P5: Validate the 30pp toxicity whitening gain at full scale (N=10187) -- if it replicates, this is a major finding

toxicity.interpretation: FM1 (space) explains 80% of Kendall tau variance. NEGATIVE sign: parameter space has HIGHER tau on toxicity.

counterfact.interpretation: FM1 explains 54% of variance. Representation space has higher tau on counterfact.

ftrace.interpretation: FM1 explains 88% of variance. Representation space has higher tau on ftrace.
